---
title: "Pipeline"
subtitle: "Architecture, installation, stages — common to every campaign"
---

The pipeline is a linear chain of stages driven by a YAML config. The same
stages and the same code are used for every campaign; only the config
changes (see the Full run section for the config used in this report). This
page documents what the pipeline is, how to install it, and what each stage
does. Campaign-specific numbers live in the per-campaign pages.

## What it does

Starting from:

- an **input source catalog** (maggies per bandpass, ra/dec, type)
- a **pointings file** carved out of `catalogs/HLWAS.sim.ecsv` by a
  cone- or box-shaped footprint filter

the pipeline produces:

- **L2 cal files**: one per (exposure × SCA), simulated with
  `romanisim-make-image` under the real CRDS reference chain.
- **L3 skycell coadds** and **associations**: Roman skycells that
  overlap the L2 footprint, coadded with `romancal.pipeline.MosaicPipeline`.
- **Source catalogs**: per-mosaic detections + photometry via
  `romancal.source_catalog.SourceCatalogStep`.
- **Recovery diagnostics**: per-skycell completeness, flux residual,
  astrometric residual, FP rate, plus an aggregated depth histogram —
  computed by `scripts/06_compare_catalog.py`.

## Installation

The project is managed by [pixi](https://pixi.sh). Once-only setup on a
fresh Linux x86_64 host:

```bash
git clone git@github.com:roman-grs-pit/roman_l2_job.git
cd roman_l2_job

# install the default env (romanisim, romancal, stpsf, etc.)
pixi install

# install the report env (quarto + plotting stack)
pixi install --environment report

# download STPSF data + scaffold CRDS directory
pixi run bash scripts/00_setup.sh

# pre-populate the CRDS reference cache (serial, avoids parallel races)
pixi run bash scripts/00_hydrate_crds.sh configs/smoke.yaml

# copy your input catalog into place (not tracked by git)
scp metadata.parquet user@host:roman_l2_job/data/

# prepare the working catalog (mag → maggies if needed)
pixi run python scripts/00_prepare_catalog.py configs/smoke.yaml
```

The env pins Python 3.12 + pinned-version romanisim, romancal, and stpsf.
See `pixi.toml` for the dependency list. BLAS threads are pinned to 1 in
the activation env so parallel `xargs -P` workers don't oversubscribe
the box.

## Pipeline shape

Every numbered stage is a shell script (or Python) taking `configs/<tag>.yaml`
as its single argument. Outputs are idempotent — each stage skips items
that already exist, so re-running picks up partial state for free.

| Stage | Script | Reads | Writes |
|---|---|---|---|
| setup | `00_setup.sh` | — | STPSF data, CRDS dir |
| hydrate CRDS | `00_hydrate_crds.sh <config>` | CRDS server | populated `crds_cache/` |
| verify CRDS | `00_verify_crds.py` | `crds_cache/` | exit nonzero on size outliers |
| catalog prep | `00_prepare_catalog.py <config>` | `catalog.input` | `catalogs/sources.parquet` (maggies) |
| filter pointings | `filter_pointings.py <config>` | `catalogs/HLWAS.sim.ecsv` | `pointings_<tag>.ecsv` |
| 01 build | `01_build_script.sh <config>` | pointings + sources | `output/<tag>/sims.script` |
| 02 sims | `02_run_sims.sh <config>` | sims.script | `output/cal/*_cal.asdf` |
| 03 asn | `03_build_asn.sh <config>` | matching cal files | `output/<tag>/asn/*.json` |
| 04 mosaic | `04_run_mosaic.sh <config>` | asn jsons | `output/<tag>/mosaic/*_coadd.asdf` |
| 05 catalog | `05_run_catalog.sh <config>` | mosaics | `output/<tag>/catalog/*_cat.parquet` + `*_segm.asdf` |
| 06 compare | `06_compare_catalog.py <config>` | sources, coadds, recovered catalogs | `output/<tag>/compare/{plots/*.png, summary.csv, depth_distribution.png}` |

Stages 02, 04, and 05 use `xargs -P $PARALLELISM` (default taken from the
config's `run.parallelism`, env var `PARALLELISM` overrides).

## Configuration

A minimal config (the actual `configs/full.yaml`):

```yaml
tag: full

catalog:
  input: data/metadata.parquet
  input_units: mag            # or "maggies" (no-op the conversion)
  bandpass_col: F158

pointings:
  region:
    type: cone                # or "box" with ra_min/max + dec_min/max
    ra: 10.0
    dec: 0.0
    radius_deg: 0.5
  bandpass: F158
  only_pass: null             # optional visit filter, null = no restriction
  only_segment: null
  only_visit: null

run:
  parallelism: 4
```

Every field is required — no hidden defaults in code. `only_pass/segment/visit`
accept integer values to carve out sub-selections (the smoke config uses
them to pick a single-visit subset for quick shake-downs).

`scripts/_config.py` is the loader. It validates the schema and exposes a
shell-export mode so bash stages can read values via
`eval "$(pixi run python scripts/_config.py $CONFIG)"`.

## Design choices

A few deliberate deviations from upstream stock `romanisim` / `romancal` worth
knowing about:

- **Vendored `make_stack.py`.** Upstream `romanisim-make-stack` unconditionally
  dereferences `apt_metadata` even in the no-APT path, which crashes the
  script when `--apt` isn't supplied. `scripts/make_stack.py` is a verbatim
  copy with one 3-line guard added. Delete when upstream merges a fix.
- **Per-line deterministic seeds.** `romanisim-make-stack --make_script`
  bakes the same `--rng_seed` into every generated line, defeating noise
  realization between dithers. `scripts/_postprocess_sims.py` rewrites
  each seed to `crc32(output_basename)` — reproducible, unique per
  (visit × exposure × SCA).
- **CRDS hydrate + verify.** The original smoke run crashed because a
  worker was OOM-killed mid-CRDS-download, leaving a truncated reference
  file that then broke every subsequent sim. `00_hydrate_crds.sh`
  pre-fetches every CRDS reference our pipeline uses (14 reftypes,
  serially via `crds.getreferences`) so the parallel stage-02 run never
  races on downloads; `00_verify_crds.py` is a size-outlier check that
  stage 02 runs as a pre-flight.
- **Parallelism.** `PARALLELISM=4` on m5a.2xlarge, not the default
  `nproc`, because the 8 vCPUs are SMT threads over 4 physical cores and
  8-way romanisim spikes to ~62 GB RAM on a 32 GB box. Stage 04 uses the
  same value for `strun MosaicPipeline` workers.
- **Footprint mask = `weight > 0`.** The recovery analysis trusts only
  sources whose (ra, dec) project onto a valid pixel of the coadd, so
  edge-of-footprint non-detections don't count against completeness.

For full operator detail (sizing, ETA, memory, troubleshooting recipes),
see `CLAUDE.md` in the repo. For file-format specifics (recovered
catalog columns, coadd array names, CRDS dataset dict shape), see
`docs/data_model_reference.md`.
